turns.content: I managed to update my Foo struct using let b = Foo { field: new_val, ..a } but I want to know if there is a better way than this syntax.

The pattern you used is the idiomatic way to do partial struct updates in Rust. It tells the compiler to take the rest of the fields from value a and only replace field with new_val. This avoids having to list every field that hasn't changed, which reduces boilerplate code.
That sounds great but I keep getting confused about whether this syntax moves or copies the struct since I am not sure if Foo implements Copy. Can you explain more about Copy vs move in update?

When using let b = Foo { field: new_val, ..a } Rust treats it as a clone operation on the fields that are not explicitly specified if a does not implement Copy. If you want to avoid cloning and instead move ownership of specific fields into b, you should use let b = { field: new_val, ..a } without the let keyword or ensure the type implements Clone so the compiler knows it can copy the rest.
